question.text: Which section of the SEBI Act gives SEBI the same powers as a civil court?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11(3) gives SEBI the same powers as a civil court under the Code of Civil Procedure for: discovery and production of documents, summoning and enforcing attendance, examining on oath, inspection of books and records, and issuing commissions for examination of witnesses. **The Trap:** Section 11(2) lists regulatory powers, 11(4) covers interim protective measures, 11B covers directions. Civil court equivalence is specifically under 11(3). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** These quasi-judicial powers make SEBI both a regulator and a quasi-judicial body — it can compel testimony under oath and force document production.
